task_completion.description: Verify that the file stock_market_data.xlsx is open in a spreadsheet application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The spreadsheet application window (e.g., Excel, LibreOffice Calc) is visible with the title bar showing 'stock_market_data.xlsx' or 'stock_market_data'.

task_completion.description: Verify that Column A (Date) and Column E (Close) are selected or explicitly used as the data source for the chart.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Columns A and E are highlighted in the spreadsheet, or the chart data range dialog shows references to Columns A and E.

task_completion.description: Verify that a Line Chart has been created to visualize the data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A chart displaying a continuous line (Line Chart) is visible on the screen, with dates on the X-axis and closing prices on the Y-axis.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Line Chart is placed on the same worksheet as the stock market data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Line Chart is floating over or placed next to the grid containing the stock market data (Columns A through F) within the same tab, not on a separate dedicated chart sheet.

task_completion.description: Verify that the changes to the file have been saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save notification, the absence of an 'unsaved changes' asterisk in the title bar, or the file menu showing a recent save action (like Ctrl+S being pressed).

integrity.description: Verify the chart was created using the spreadsheet application's native GUI tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: The chart is embedded within the spreadsheet application interface (e.g., Excel/Calc), not displayed in an external image viewer, web browser, or Python plotting window.

integrity.description: Verify the chart reflects the actual provided stock data and is not a generic pasted image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The chart's Y-axis values should align with the AAPL data (ranging roughly between 160 and 220), starting near 184 and ending near 209, with the X-axis showing dates from January to June 2024.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use a terminal or script to bypass GUI interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows, command prompts, or code editors (like VS Code) open running Python/pandas/matplotlib scripts to generate the chart or modify the Excel file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains AAPL historical stock prices from January 2, 2024 to June 28, 2024. Column A is Date and Column E is Close. The closing price starts at approximately 183.90 on Jan 2 and ends at approximately 209.20 on Jun 28. The generated Line Chart must reflect this specific trend and value range (roughly 160 to 220 on the Y-axis).
